P·WHIFF: Green=above LG avg (pitcher advantage) · Red=below LG avg. MATCH = pitcher whiff × (opp÷LG) — how well this pitch works against this lineup
K%/Whiff% = batter actual rate vs this pitcher's exact pitch mix, weighted by usage. Green = pitcher advantage · Red = pitcher disadvantage. Coverage dot: ●high ●mod ●low ●no data
Signal
Over 8.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 10.0K vs 8.5. 64% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Misiorowski generates genuine swing-and-miss (34% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. FF is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. ATL is roughly neutral — 23.2% MIX-K close to league average. Moderate variance (σ2.2K) — no major consistency flags.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 6.5 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓FF converts two-strike counts (26% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Elite swing-and-miss stuff (34% WHF) — Ks can stack quickly when he's on
Bet loses if
✗Variance on any given start (σ2.2K) — projection is a median, not a floor

Signal
Over 5.5 is the play.1.5 units
Solid over edge at 6.9K vs 5.5. 65% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Skubal generates genuine swing-and-miss (30% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. FF is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. CWS is an excellent matchup — 27.6% MIX-K well above LG. Lineup swings against his mix. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 6.0 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓FF converts two-strike counts (26% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Sharp command (1.3 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
Bet loses if
✗Variance on any given start (σ2.0K) — projection is a median, not a floor

Lean over 6.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Marginal over edge (7.2K vs 6.5), 57% conviction — take it reduced.
deGrom generates genuine swing-and-miss (32% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. FF is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. SD is a good matchup — 26.3% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.
Bet wins if
✓FF converts two-strike counts (28% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓Sharp command (1.8 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
✓SD swings vs his specific mix (26.3% MIX-K) — lineup is genuinely exploitable
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.7 IP — short outings cap K upside hard

Lean over 5.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Marginal over edge (6.1K vs 5.5), 55% conviction — take it reduced.
Miller is above average on whiff rate (26%) — solid enough to capitalize when lineup cooperates. FF is a serviceable finishing pitch, not dominant in two-strike counts. BOS is a good matchup — 25.0% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. Moderate variance (σ2.0K) — no major consistency flags.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 6.0 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓Sharp command (1.3 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
✓BOS swings vs his specific mix (25.0% MIX-K) — lineup is genuinely exploitable
Bet loses if
✗FF is serviceable not dominant (22% putaway) — won't blow batters away

P·WHIFF: Green=above LG avg (pitcher advantage) · Red=below LG avg. MATCH = pitcher whiff × (opp÷LG) — how well this pitch works against this lineup
K%/Whiff% = batter actual rate vs this pitcher's exact pitch mix, weighted by usage. Green = pitcher advantage · Red = pitcher disadvantage. Coverage dot: ●high ●mod ●low ●no data
Signal
Lean under 4.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Under edge present (3.1K vs 4.5), 25% confidence — lean only.
Jax sits around league average on whiff rate (24%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. WSH is roughly neutral — 23.5% MIX-K close to league average. Moderate variance (σ1.6K) — no major consistency flags.
Bet wins if
✓Short average outing (3.9 IP) — fewer PA limits total K ceiling
Bet loses if
✗K/9 of 8.4 means the ceiling exists — a long outing can push over the number
✗Low FPS (56%) — he works from behind, counts go deep, Ks can build late

Model projects 5.7K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Sasaki generates genuine swing-and-miss (30% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. FF is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. BAL is a good matchup — 26.1% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. Moderate variance (σ2.2K) — no major consistency flags.

Chandler sits around league average on whiff rate (24%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. Lugo sits around league average on whiff rate (22%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. STL is a good matchup — 26.0% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. Very consistent output (σ1.5K) — model projection is reliable here.

Model projects 4.7K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Brown generates genuine swing-and-miss (30% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. SL is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. TOR is a mild headwind — 21.0% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.

8Yelich, C.L15.8%15%~33%32%~50%
9Ortiz, J.R9.1%12%—~4%—
LINEUP AVG12.5%whiff% vs pitcher's mix · green=vulnerable · red=contact
K% = weighted vs pitch mix · Whiff% = batter vs that pitch type · ⭐ = putaway pitch · Dot: ●confirmed ●projected ●DTD
PITCH MATCHUP
PITCHUSAGEP·WHIFFOPP.WHFMATCH
SI
42%
18.0%11.2%14.7%
CH
28%
22.0%32.6%22.8%
FC
18%
20.0%22.4%19.6%
CU
12%
16.0%27.8%14.4%
MATCH:ELITE >40%GOOD 25-40%AVG 18-25%WEAK <18%
P·WHIFF: Green=above LG avg (pitcher advantage) · Red=below LG avg. MATCH = pitcher whiff × (opp÷LG) — how well this pitch works against this lineup
K%/Whiff% = batter actual rate vs this pitcher's exact pitch mix, weighted by usage. Green = pitcher advantage · Red = pitcher disadvantage. Coverage dot: ●high ●mod ●low ●no data
Signal
Pass.Pass
Model projects 3.6K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Pérez is below average on swing-and-miss (20% WHF vs 25% LG) — needs favorable conditions to reach Ks. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. MIL is a good matchup — 24.5% MIX-K above LG, lineup vulnerability is a real tailwind. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.
Real FD Lines · model=3.6 · BP +115
LineFD OddsP(over)EV%
O1.5-1371bpO -696-6.2%

O3.5-192bpO +106-26.3%
O4.5 ·+ 115bpO +241-36.9%
O5.5+ 246bpO +541-46.1%

Trey Gibson λ0.62 (P0R 54%) · Roki Sasaki λ0.47 (P0R 63%) · Combined P(NRFI) 33.6% · Park 0.94 · OBP —/— · Poisson model
Model v9.5
K Props · F5 · NRFI · methodology + weightings · v9.5 edge% tiering
⚡ K Props — Strikeout Projection v9.2
L10-based, 7-factor formula. Each L10 start normalized by opponent K rate: adj_k = raw_k × (22.7 / opp_K%). Base = adj_L10_avg × 0.70 + k9_proj × 0.30. K/9 talent is embedded in base — no separate term, no double-count. All 7 factors are ratios vs league average × base × weight.
Adj L10 base · opp-normalized · 70% L10 + 30% K/9 in base
32%
Whiff% vs lg avg (25%) — strongest Statcast K predictor
22%
Opp K% vs lg avg (22.7%) — today's matchup context
18%
Arsenal quality · ELITE 1.18 · STRONG 1.09 · MOD 1.00 · WEAK 0.88
11%
Putaway% vs lg avg (19%) — 2-strike whiff rate
9%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command drives deep counts
6%
Park factor inverted (2.0 − park) — small but real
2%
Strong ≥1.5K edge · Moderate 0.5–1.4K · Under ≤−1.2K · Pass <0.5K edge
🔴 F5 — First 5 Innings Run Projection
ERA base blended 60% pitcher ERA + 40% league average (4.3R F5). Prevents extreme suppression from elite starters. wRC+ is hand-adjusted (vs RHP or LHP). L10 R/G captures recent offensive form. WHIP captures baserunner risk beyond strikeouts. IP capped at 5.0 for F5.
L5 F5 ERA base × IP × park adj
35%
Lineup wRC+ vs pitcher handedness (lg avg 100)
25%
Opponent L10 R/G vs lg avg (4.35)
20%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command = fewer baserunners
12%
WHIP vs lg avg (1.20) — baserunner rate
8%
📋 Full Game — Complete Game Run Projection
ERA base blended 60% pitcher ERA + 40% league average (8.7R full game). SP and bullpen each weighted separately. wRC+ hand-adjusted. Park factor applied to both teams. L10 R/G for offensive form. FG uses 5.5 IP default vs 5.0 for F5.
Starter ERA vs lg avg (4.50) × proj innings
30%
Bullpen ERA vs lg avg (4.20) × remaining innings
25%
Lineup wRC+ vs pitcher handedness (lg avg 100)
20%
Park factor — direct multiplier on run environment
15%
Opponent L10 R/G vs lg avg (4.35)
10%
🎯 NRFI / YRFI — First Inning Scoring
Poisson model. Expected runs per inning = (opp L10 R/G ÷ 9) adjusted by ERA, FPS%, and WHIP. P(0 runs) = e^(−exp_r) per team. Combined NRFI = away_prob × home_prob. Higher FPS + lower ERA + tougher lineup = closer to NRFI.
SP ERA vs lg avg (4.50) — primary scoring suppressor
~35%
FPS% vs lg avg (63%) — command = fewer baserunners in 1st
~30%
WHIP vs lg avg (1.20) — baserunner risk
~20%
Opponent L10 R/G ÷ 9 — per-inning run environment
~15%
NRFI ≥62% · NRFI lean 57–61% · Skip 43–56% · YRFI ≤42%
Top 4 lineup OBP shown with individual player names. Danger ≥.350 avg OBP · Watch ≥.330 · Manageable <.330
📐 Implied Odds Box
Poisson distribution (λ = proj K) implied odds for 4 alt line thresholds. O% + U% = 100%. Bold row = closest to the FanDuel line. Compare to FanDuel alt lines to find additional edge.
P(K ≥ n) = 1 − Σ(k=0 to n−1) [e^−λ × λ^k / k!]
American odds: P≥50% → −round(P/(1−P)×100)
📡 Data Sources
✓ Real (API) — L10 K counts · pitch counts · season ERA/K9/BB9/WHIP · FanDuel K prop lines + odds
⚠ Researched — Arsenal whiff% · FPS% · putaway% · matchup K rates · park factors · bullpen ERA · L5 F5 ERA · lineup OBP + wRC+ · top 4 threats
✗ Gaps — L10 opponents (API returns ? — normalization falls back to raw avg) · live Statcast splits · real-time lineup changes
